channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Xbox Revenue Falls In Latest Microsoft Earnings Report
item.content:encoded:
Xbox revenue has dipped from the previous quarter, according to Microsoft’s latest earnings report.

On April 29, 2026, Microsoft released its third-quarter fiscal year 2026 earnings report. When examining the Xbox-specific portions of the report, a decrease in revenue is evident in the first quarter following Phil Spencer’s retirement and Asha Sharma’s appointment as head of the division. Of course, as Sharma only took over in February, neither her impact nor the strategies she’s begun to implement are reflected here.

Xbox Revenue Is Down 10% From Previous Quarter, Per Earnings Report

The information provided by Microsoft notes that Xbox content and services revenue declined 6.6% year over year. Xbox revenue was $5.34 billion, down from $5.72 billion at this time last year. There was a 10.4% decrease from the previous quarter, when the division’s revenue closed at $5.96 billion.

Consistent growth is a key pillar issue for Asha Sharma. Part of her strategy to revitalize the Xbox brand is simply that: restoring the brand name. Sharma and Matt Booty, brand CCO, recently put out a joint memo discussing how, moving forward, they would be moving away from the Microsoft Gaming branding and focusing solely on the Xbox name. Sharma is eyeing the next 12 months as a significant timeframe that, she hopes, will see Xbox return to growth.

“We are wanting to see Xbox return to growth next year, and so we’ve got work to do,” she’d say in an interview with Steven Totillo of Game File. “There’s no silver bullets, and our focus is going to be: how many players are playing every single day in the Xbox ecosystem?”

Xbox hardware revenue decreased 33% year over year, consistent with the previous quarter’s declines. Overall, Microsoft’s total brand revenue increased 18% to $82.9 billion. For additional details on the company’s third quarter of the fiscal year, click here.

For more Xbox news, check out what Asha Sharma has had to say about affordability for players in the near future. Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: LEC EWC Qualifier Round 2 Recap: Vitality Throw Massive Lead
item.content:encoded:
The second round of the Esports World Cup qualifiers for EMEA League of Legends teams has concluded with the rest of the LEC joining the fray.

The top four from LEC Versus played their first matches of the qualifiers as teams try to qualify for the lucrative $2m LoL tournament in Riyadh later this Summer.

There was a lot of action, including a massive comeback for Movistar KOI versus Team Vitality and a great showing from G2 Esports.

Here’s how the second round of the EMEA LoL Esports World Cup Qualifiers played out.

G2 Esports Get Revenge on Fnatic

G2 Esports got off to winning ways in the Esports World Cup with a strong 2-0 win over Fnatic.

Game 1 was all about the mid jungle duos, as the game opened up with both teams trading successive mid ganks.

However, in the long run, it was Rasmus ‘Caps’ Winther who came out on top.

Just 15 minutes in, G2 managed to find an ace, which took Caps’ Ahri to a 1.8k gold lead over Vladimiros ‘Vladi’ Kourtidis.

G2 took that first game, but Fnatic’s Iván ‘Razork’ Martín Díaz was still showing excellent form.

The Spanish jungler was smurfing on Naafiri, finding multiple kills early to give Fnatic a lead in Game 2.

G2 managed to respond by sneaking the third Drake to take away Soul Point, and they made it out for free as Park ‘Lospa’ Joon-hyeong’s Bard ultimate was too late for the Dragon and only trapped Razork.

Fnatic were playing skirmishes well, but G2’s macro play was much better. The reigning LEC champions played the map beautifully to corner Fnatic and win the series 2-0.

Naak Nako vs Movistar KOI

Team Vitality have been in fine form in the LEC, but against MKOI the team showed that they still have a lot to work on.

Most of the series came down to the entirety of MKOI versus Vitality’s in-form top laner, Kaan ‘Naak Nako’ Okan.

Naak Nako was stomping top, solokilling Alex ‘Myrwn’ Villarejo twice in lane in the first match and three times early into the second.

MKOI were still able to win the first match, as Javier ‘Elyoya’ Prades’ Pantheon took advantage of his jungle opponent AFK farming early on.

Then in Game 2, Elyoya and Joseph ‘Jojopyun’ Pyun were still causing Vitality issues, but Naak Nako and Matyáš ‘Carzzy’ Orság were too far ahead, and the damage from the dual ADCs in Vayne and Lucian was too much to handle.

The last match was then a ridiculous 40+ minute battle.

Jojopyun was 6/1/2 on Anivia and 2k up over Marek ‘Humanoid’ Brázda, but the rest of MKOI were behind their positional countreparts.

Yet, it looked like MKOI were about to get Soul and find a way back, but then allowed Linas “Lyncas” Nauncikas to Smite steal it away.

Vitality then aced the next team fight with Carzzy’s Corki dealing ridiculous damage, and it looked like the series was over.

However, despite Vitality’s 12k gold lead, an overstep by Humanoid allowed MKOI to win a team fight then take both Baron and Soul.

MKOI were trying to end and complete a remarkable comeback, but Vitality were able to hold their base and remain 7k gold up.

41 minutes into the game, Vitality rushed Baron and barely secured it, but David ‘Supa’ García solo’d Elder which allowed his team to pick off Vitality’s players and finally end the game.

NAVI remain undefeated

Natus Vincere (NAVI) has still not dropped a game in the Esports World Cup Qualifiers.

After sweeping Galions the day before, NAVI were just as dominant versus GIANTX.

NAVI ADC Lee ‘SamD’ Jae-hoon had struggled against Galions’ bot lane a day prior, but responded perfectly against GIANTX.

He finished the first game with a KDA of 11/2/5 and 12k gold up on Oh ‘Noah’ Hyeon-taek.

Afterwards, GIANTX found themselves 2.5k gold down just seven minutes into the game as NAVI fully found their rhythm.

Unlike previous weeks, NAVI had no trouble finishing the game and recorded another 2-0.

Karmine Corp Sweep SK Gaming

Karmine Corp eased past SK Gaming 2-0 to progress through the upper bracket.

The first game was a total stomp as KCorp were almost 5k gold up with 3 Drakes by the 20 minute-mark.

However, SK Gaming and new midlaner Seo ‘SlowQ’ Ye-bit held their own in Game 2.

SK were seven kills up to three, but KCorp were leading in gold solely because Kim ‘Canna’ Chang-dong was 3k up over Martin ‘Wunder’ Hansen.

KCorp eventually took control of the game and picked up a 6k gold lead, yet SK secured Infernal Soul with a steal to make a possible comeback.

However, SK still couldn’t find a way to win fights and dropped down into the Lower Bracket.

The Next Matches

The LoL EWC EMEA bracket now looks like this:

  • Upper Bracket
    • G2 vs NAVI
    • KCorp vs MKOI
  • Lower Bracket (elimination games)
    • Team Vitality vs Shifters
    • SK Gaming vs Galions
    • GIANTX vs Team Heretics
    • Fnatic vs Solary

Who is the strongest LoL team in EMEA right now? Share your opinions with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, outside of the EWC Qualifiers, NAVI and KCorp have qualified for the LEC Spring Playoffs.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/lec-ewc-qualifier-round-2-recap/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: M80 Benches Hotancold After Failing to Qualify for Salt Lake City Major
item.content:encoded:
M80 has made a drastic and sudden move, benching Matthew ‘Hotancold’ Stevens after the organization failed to qualify for the BLAST R6 Salt Lake City Major. The IGL had been in the spot since being picked up in March 2025, assuming control of the M80 banner alongside his championship-winning beastcoast teammates.

In the last two years, Hotancold stormed to success at the BLAST R6 Major Manchester and the BLAST R6 Major Munich (just a few months ago), which he mentioned when venting his frustrations recently on social media.

Hotancold Doesn’t Understand M80’s Bench Spot

On social media, M80 released a statement regarding the benching of Hotancold, a two-time Major winner and eight-year veteran of the game:

Since stepping int othe IGL role in March 2025, Matt led us to a Major championship in Munich and helped define what this team could be. He has been an integral part of the success we’ve had together, and we can’t thank him enough for his contributions.

Moving forward, we will be trialing players in the coming weeks and will share more news once these trials are complete.

A proper goodbye will come in time. For now – thank you, Matt. We’re wishing you nothing but success wherever your journey takes you next.

The fan backlash was almost immediate, with one user spouting: ‘Benching the only NA IGL to win an event (let alone two) in years is certainly a choice.’

Hotancold then posted twelve minutes later, stunned by the news:

Woke up and am now benched, obviously haven’t been playing the best lately but I think this move is made without thinking what we made with beastcoast/m80. (The ONLY NA team to win anything in 4 years). Initial thought is I still want to play obviously, but we will see what’s next

Three days ago, M80 failed to clinch qualification to the Salt Lake City Major. They fell out of the running in seventh place in the NAL Kickoff, and instead, the four spots went to:

  • Five Fears
  • Wildcard
  • DarkZero
  • Shopify Rebellion

Do you think M80 has made a weird move by benching Hotancold? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Fnatic has unveiled a skin gambling site

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: Fnatic Unveils Skin Gambling Site ‘STASHD’ and Gets Hit With Widespread Backlash
item.content:encoded:
After a month of teasers posted on social media, Fnatic has unveiled STASHD, a Counter-Strike skin gambling and trading platform that gives ‘fans and collectors a fun and transparent way to collect skins.’ Two days ago, the STASHD platform was uncovered in full, prompting intense backlash from Fnatic’s community.

This is by no means a new concept in the Counter-Strike world, as case gambling, skin trading, and skin betting sites are a dime a dozen, but Fnatic’s fans have accused the team of brutalizing an already weakening brand name with this platform.

Fnatic’s Fans Slam STASHD Opening

On social media, a Fnatic fan club with thousands of followers publicly condemned STASHD, suggesting the mere opening of the site is not something they can support:

We’re deeply disappointed by the direction Fnatic has taken.

An org we’ve supported for years is now attaching its name to a platform built around a framework which reminds of gambling adjacent practices. That’s not something we can stand behind.

We want to be clear, this is not about resisting change or growth. It is about protecting a community that includes many young and impressionable fans.

We strongly condemn this type of practice.

Unfortunately, securing profitability in the esports industry is still a tricky topic, and few organizations are in the green these days. This is why a lot of these larger teams turn to gambling, questionable sponsorships, and, at times, dubious activations to bolster their income and revenue streams

It’s also no big secret that esports and gambling go together like peanut butter and jelly. Around half of the industry’s revenue comes from esports betting.

The comments under the above post were vehemently against the surfacing of STASHD. One user wrote, ‘This is what happens when the org has been run into the ground by incompetent leadership.’ Another said, ‘Fnatic would literally rather chase gambling money in CS than actually take care of their own CS team.’

One user echoed the money-making sentiment:

Promoting gambling sucks, but esports orgs don’t have many other ways to make money. If you’re not already running world class rosters or backed by investors with deep pockets, it’s tough to win or pull fans.

For now Fnatic is promoting a sign-up bonus with STASHD, giving users a free case when they register for a new account.

Do you think Fnatic is making the wrong move with STASHD? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Cache is back in CS2

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Injustice 3 in the Works at NetherRealm Studios, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
NetherRealm Studios is reportedly working on the third game in the DC Comics fighting franchise, Injustice.

It’s been nearly 10 years since the release of the last Injustice game. The series, which pits DC Superheroes against one another, is produced by the Mortal Kombat development team. It is known for its storyline that explores an alternate universe in which Superman becomes a rogue global dictator after being tricked by the Joker into killing Lois Lane and their unborn child. Eventually, the Justice League from a more typical universe would step in to stop the maniacal “Man of Steel.”

The storyline has been adapted into comic books and an animated film. But now, it may be time for the next chapter in the video game series, according to a new report.

Injustice 3 Is in Development, Per An Artist’s Resume

According to a report by MP1st, the game is currently in development. The information they shared comes from the resume of an anonymous artist currently working at Warner Bros. Games. The artist’s identity was withheld for confidentiality reasons. However, additional projects they are working on include the sequel to Hogwarts Legacy and two projects that are labeled unannounced.

In 2022, Ed Boon, one of the original creators of Mortal Kombat and the head of NetherRealm Studios, announced his intention to return to the series. In November of that year, Boon tweeted out that his next project would be the next Injustice title or Mortal Kombat 12. The latter would eventually release as Mortal Kombat 1 in 2023. The game served as a reboot for the MK series following Mortal Kombat 11.

With nearly 3 years since that game’s release, NetherRealm Studios and Warner Bros. Games would theoretically be in the clear to work hard on the next Injustice release. Still, only time will tell.

Are you ready for Injustice 3? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1776: Hints and Answer for April 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1776 for April 30, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, April 30, 2026

Today’s Wordle answer for game #1776 is challenging. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 5.3 out of 6 guesses. That’s a bit harder than the 4.8-guess average from April 29.

General Wordle Hints For April 30

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: It refers to a sturdy container often used for storage.
  • Hint 2: Traditionally made from a heavy material and used in kitchens.
  • Hint 3: You might associate it with preserving or holding food.
  • Hint 4: The word can also be used informally to mean nonsense.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1776 for April 30, 2026.

Starter Words For April 30, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • ROCKY
  • CROWN
  • CORKS
  • CORNY
  • BROOK

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1776 On April 30, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: CROCK

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

CROCK is a noun that means “an earthenware pot, jar, or other container” or “a person or thing that is old, decrepit, or broken-down.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1775 on April 29, 2026

Answer: RURAL

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1776

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on May 1 will be Wordle Game #1777.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Invincible VS Launch Patch Tweaks Move-Sets and Addresses Crashes
item.content:encoded:
With Invincible VS set to launch on April 30, the development team has shared details about the day-one patch and what players can expect. The upcoming fighting game is set to put its own violent and gory spin on the traditional 3-on-3 tag fighter. It will, of course, come with a loaded day-one patch, like most video games in 2026.

The day one patch for Invincible VS takes several notes from the open beta held in mid-April, and several courses have now been adjusted. There are also major adjustments to the fighters’ movesets, as well as general bug fixes.

Key Takeaways From Invincible VS Day One Patch

The patch notes state that an overall pass has been done to damage scaling to reduce the number of “Touch of Death routes utilizing easy-to-perform/repeatable combos. Also, a pass has been performed on every Fighter’s Special, Boosted Special, and Super moves that are intended to make them unsafe to block and open to punish.

As for fighter changes, the main focus was ensuring there were more of the aforementioned punish windows on special and super moves. Additionally, every fighter on the roster has received tuning, such as Omni-Man’s Rising Uppercut now being punishable on block.

Other General Bug Fixes from Invincible VS Patch

The patch also includes generic bug fixes, such as resolving more than 80% of the crashes observed in the open beta. Also, the ghost player issue in online matchmaking has been fixed.

The development team is still working on known issues, such as adding a cooldown penalty for players who leave Ranked Matches early, with this update aimed for release during the first major patch after launch. Check out the full patch notes here.

For more Invincible VS, check out the details of the offline story mode.

Are you excited for this upcoming fighting game? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
